report_name: State-Sponsored Ransomware Blurs Lines, Increasingly Deployed as Proxy Weapon Against Critical Infrastructure

threat_actors.name: MuddyWater
threat_actors.description: An Iran-aligned APT group reported to be leveraging criminal ransomware gangs as proxies to attack critical infrastructure and Operational Technology (OT) environments, shifting from pure espionage to disruptive sabotage operations.
threat_actors.goals: Cause physical disruption and exert geopolitical pressure while maintaining plausible deniability.

threat_actors.name: APT33
threat_actors.description: An Iran-aligned APT group collaborating with criminal outfits to deploy ransomware against critical infrastructure, focusing on OT environments to cause kinetic effects.
threat_actors.goals: Cause physical disruption and exert geopolitical pressure while maintaining plausible deniability.

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: A criminal ransomware outfit reportedly collaborating with or acting as a proxy for Iran-aligned state actors like MuddyWater and APT33 to attack critical infrastructure.
threat_actors.goals: Disruption of critical infrastructure, potentially for financial gain as a secondary objective.

threat_actors.name: Handala Hack
threat_actors.description: A hacktivist group reportedly collaborating with or acting as a proxy for Iran-aligned state actors to conduct disruptive attacks against critical infrastructure sectors.
threat_actors.goals: Disruption of critical infrastructure, likely for ideological or geopolitical reasons.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used as a common initial access vector by Iran-aligned actors to penetrate target networks before moving to OT environments.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware is used to encrypt Human-Machine Interfaces (HMIs), engineering workstations, and data historians to directly inhibit the ability of operators to monitor and control industrial processes.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Process Control
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware attacks on OT systems are used to inhibit operators' ability to monitor and control industrial processes, aiming for kinetic effects.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware behaviors include deleting shadow copies to prevent easy recovery of encrypted files.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Used as part of living-off-the-land techniques by attackers after gaining access to the network.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Used by attackers to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used as a living-off-the-land tool for executing commands and scripts within the compromised environment.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure
identities.description: Critical infrastructure and Operational Technology (OT) sectors targeted by Iran-aligned threat actors using ransomware for disruptive purposes.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b9a8bbf-7a8d-4b8e-9c0a-3d2e1f0a9b8c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/threats-attacks/state-backed-ransomware-activity-raises-new-concerns-over-escalating-threats-to-ot-critical-infrastructure-operations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-18
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of vssadmin.exe to delete shadow copies, a technique associated with ransomware to prevent recovery.

indicators.name: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
id: 5c3d2e1f-0a9b-4c8d-8bbf-7a8d4b8e9c0a
status: experimental
description: Detects potentially malicious lateral movement from IT networks to sensitive OT networks, which is a key step in attacks against critical infrastructure.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/threats-attacks/state-backed-ransomware-activity-raises-new-concerns-over-escalating-threats-to-ot-critical-infrastructure-operations/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-18
tags:
  - attack.t1570
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    SrcIp|cidr:
      - '192.168.0.0/16' # Example IT network range
      - '10.0.0.0/8' # Example IT network range
    DestinationIp|cidr:
      - '172.16.0.0/12' # Example OT network range
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects unusual data flows or unauthorized protocols between IT and OT networks, indicating potential lateral movement by an attacker.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
